header.location: Portland, OR (Open to Remote or Relocation)

summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) HR Generalist and Organizational Development professional who thrives on keeping HRIS data accurate and employee operations running smoothly, developing people programs grounded in insights, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve employee experience—through human-centered, systematic approaches.

Empower HR to present executive-ready insights on $362K in leave costs, leading to policy redesigns and first aid training
Establish {cross|cross-functional} processes and documentation for 70+ staff, streamlining {coord|operations} and reducing {prog|onboarding} time by 75%

changeLog.note: Tailored for Aurora Innovation HR Generalist (autonomous vehicle / tech company). Summary anchored on 'HR Generalist' + 'Organizational Development' dual identity per CLAUDE.md instructions. Key changes: (1) Summary: added 'HR Generalist' title label + HRIS data accuracy framing. (2) InGenius: Set H bullets — cross-functional processes/documentation, onboarding 75% anchor, engagement research for HR; dropped learning-gaps framing. (3) Vestas: HR variant — M&A record migration leads (direct HRIS data accuracy match), streamlined onboarding across 3 locations, employee relations + compliance. Newsletter bullet replaced with ER/compliance bullet. (4) Skills: Workday-user listed first (JD's preferred HRIS), kept SAP as secondary signal; dropped LMS, dropped AI Tools (not relevant for operational HR). (5) NextGen: standard 3 bullets retained. (6) Kronos: trimmed to 2 bullets for page fit. Prior Aurora PM People Team application acknowledged in cover letter only.
